items.name: Alabama
facts: Alabama was the 22nd state to join the United States.
Rocket science work in Huntsville helped space missions.

items.name: Alaska
facts: Alaska is the largest U.S. state by area.
It has very long summer days in some places.

items.name: Arizona
facts: The Grand Canyon is one of Arizona's famous landmarks.
Much of Arizona has a dry desert climate.

items.name: Arkansas
facts: Arkansas has mountains, rivers, and hot springs.
Little Rock is its capital city.

items.name: California
facts: California is the most populated U.S. state.
It is home to both beaches and giant redwood trees.

items.name: Colorado
facts: Colorado is known for the Rocky Mountains.
Denver is called the Mile High City.

items.name: Connecticut
facts: Connecticut is one of the original 13 colonies.
Hartford is one of the oldest U.S. cities.

items.name: Delaware
facts: Delaware was the first state to ratify the U.S. Constitution.
It is one of the smallest U.S. states.

items.name: Florida
facts: Florida is surrounded by water on three sides.
The Everglades are a famous wetland in Florida.

items.name: Georgia
facts: Georgia is known for peaches and peanuts.
Atlanta is a major city in the Southeast.

items.name: Hawaii
facts: Hawaii is the only U.S. state made entirely of islands.
Volcanoes helped form the Hawaiian islands.

items.name: Idaho
facts: Idaho is famous for potatoes.
The state has mountains, forests, and rivers.

items.name: Illinois
facts: Illinois is home to Chicago, one of the biggest U.S. cities.
Abraham Lincoln spent many important years in Illinois.

items.name: Indiana
facts: Indianapolis hosts the famous Indy 500 race.
Indiana has many farms and small towns.

items.name: Iowa
facts: Iowa is known for corn and farmland.
Its rolling fields are part of the Midwest landscape.

items.name: Kansas
facts: Kansas has wide plains and prairie land.
Sunflowers are an important state symbol.

items.name: Kentucky
facts: Kentucky is known for bluegrass music and horse farms.
Mammoth Cave is one of the world's longest cave systems.

items.name: Louisiana
facts: Louisiana is famous for jazz and Mardi Gras traditions.
The Mississippi River flows through the state.

items.name: Maine
facts: Maine is known for rocky coasts and lighthouses.
It is the easternmost state in the United States.

items.name: Maryland
facts: Maryland borders the Chesapeake Bay.
Blue crabs are a well-known food in Maryland.

items.name: Massachusetts
facts: Massachusetts played a big role in early American history.
Boston is one of the oldest major cities in the country.

items.name: Michigan
facts: Michigan touches four of the five Great Lakes.
It has two main land areas called peninsulas.

items.name: Minnesota
facts: Minnesota is known as the Land of 10,000 Lakes.
It has cold winters and many forests.

items.name: Mississippi
facts: The Mississippi River helps give the state its name.
Magnolia trees are an important state symbol.

items.name: Missouri
facts: Missouri is home to the Gateway Arch in St. Louis.
The Missouri and Mississippi rivers meet near the state.

items.name: Montana
facts: Montana has wide-open spaces and mountain views.
Part of Yellowstone National Park reaches into Montana.

items.name: Nebraska
facts: Nebraska has large plains and farmland.
It is an important state for corn and beef production.

items.name: Nevada
facts: Nevada has deserts, mountains, and bright city lights in Las Vegas.
The state is known for silver mining history.

items.name: New Hampshire
facts: New Hampshire is known for mountains and forests.
It is one of the original 13 states.

items.name: New Jersey
facts: New Jersey has beaches, farms, and busy cities.
It played an important part in the American Revolution.

items.name: New Mexico
facts: New Mexico has deserts, mesas, and colorful art traditions.
Santa Fe is one of the oldest capital cities in the U.S.

items.name: New York
facts: New York City is the largest city in the United States.
Niagara Falls is one of the state's famous natural wonders.

items.name: North Carolina
facts: North Carolina has both mountains and Atlantic beaches.
The Wright brothers made early flight history at Kitty Hawk.

items.name: North Dakota
facts: North Dakota has prairies and grasslands.
It shares a border with Canada.

items.name: Ohio
facts: Ohio is known for buckeye trees and important inventions.
Several U.S. presidents were born in Ohio.

items.name: Oklahoma
facts: Oklahoma has strong Native American history and culture.
It is part of a region known for big skies and plains.

items.name: Oregon
facts: Oregon has forests, mountains, and Pacific coastline.
Crater Lake is one of the deepest lakes in the U.S.

items.name: Pennsylvania
facts: Pennsylvania is where the Liberty Bell is found.
Philadelphia played a major role in U.S. history.

items.name: Rhode Island
facts: Rhode Island is the smallest U.S. state.
It has many bays, harbors, and seaside towns.

items.name: South Carolina
facts: South Carolina has beaches, marshes, and historic cities.
Palmetto trees are an important state symbol.

items.name: South Dakota
facts: Mount Rushmore is one of South Dakota's most famous landmarks.
The state has prairies and the Black Hills.

items.name: Tennessee
facts: Tennessee is well known for country music in Nashville.
The Great Smoky Mountains are partly in Tennessee.

items.name: Texas
facts: Texas is the second-largest U.S. state by area.
It has deserts, forests, beaches, and big cities.

items.name: Utah
facts: Utah is known for red rock landscapes and national parks.
The Great Salt Lake is one of its famous natural features.

items.name: Vermont
facts: Vermont is known for maple syrup and green mountains.
Montpelier is the smallest U.S. state capital by population.

items.name: Virginia
facts: Virginia is one of the original 13 colonies.
It has beaches, mountains, and important historic sites.

items.name: Washington
facts: Washington is known for evergreen forests and rainy weather in some areas.
Seattle is one of its biggest cities.

items.name: West Virginia
facts: West Virginia is filled with mountains and forests.
Outdoor adventures like rafting are popular there.

items.name: Wisconsin
facts: Wisconsin is famous for dairy farms and cheese.
It borders two Great Lakes.

items.name: Wyoming
facts: Wyoming was the first state to allow women to vote.
Much of Yellowstone National Park is in Wyoming.
